complexity.justification: Single-file sequential read/display with one error-handling branch; no writes, no transformation

annotation_metadata.note: source_lines and cobol_snippet fields checked against the uploaded VSCBEX02.cbl

rules.name: BR_001
rules.confidence_justification: Direct OPEN statement plus an explicit priming read call
rules.natural_language_rule: The program opens the indexed customer file for sequential input and reads the first record before entering the main processing loop.
rules.precondition: INFile is defined in FILE-CONTROL and not yet opened
rules.action: OPEN INFile for INPUT, then perform the priming read
rules.postcondition: INFile is open and the first record (or EOF) is loaded

rules.name: BR_002
rules.confidence_justification: Inline PERFORM...UNTIL is explicit COBOL loop control
rules.natural_language_rule: Every record in the indexed customer file is processed and displayed in sequence until end-of-file is reached.
rules.precondition: The priming read from BR_001 has completed
rules.action: Repeatedly perform 2000-Process until WS-INFile-EOF is TRUE
rules.postcondition: All readable records have been displayed; WS-INFile-EOF is TRUE

rules.name: BR_003
rules.cobol_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-INFile-Good
       DISPLAY INFile-Customer-Record
    END-IF.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-INFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: If the most recent read was successful, the current customer record is written to the console, then the next record is read to continue the loop.
rules.precondition: A read (priming or prior loop iteration) has just completed
rules.action: DISPLAY the record when the read status is good, then read the next record
rules.postcondition: Record is shown to the operator; the next record or EOF is loaded

rules.name: TR_004
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; unambiguous counter increment
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the record-read counter by 1 each time a record is successfully read from the indexed file.
rules.postcondition: FD-INFile-Record-Cnt reflects the number of records read so far

rules.name: TR_005
rules.confidence_justification: Direct DISPLAY statements for the run summary
rules.cobol_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "   Records Read: " FD-INFile-Record-Cnt
rules.natural_language_rule: At end of processing, the program displays an end-of-job banner and the total count of records read for operator visibility.
rules.action: DISPLAY end-of-job banner and read count
rules.postcondition: Summary information is visible to the operator

rules.name: TR_006
rules.natural_language_rule: The indexed customer file is closed before program termination to release the file handle.

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ INFile
    AT END SET WS-INFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-INFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-INFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-INFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-INFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a read of the indexed file fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately.
rules.precondition: A READ against INFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally
